title: Selective adsorbent of disease mediators
abstract: A carbonaceous material comprising at least 80 wt. % pure carbon and a functional group, wherein the functional group has been chemically-associated with the carbonaceous material by ultrasonication. A method comprising obtaining an uncleansed bodily fluid from a subject; contacting the uncleansed bodily fluid with a carbonaceous material comprising at least 80 wt. % pure carbon and a functional group, wherein the functional group has been chemically-associated with the carbonaceous material by ultrasonication; obtaining a cleansed bodily fluid subsequent to the contacting; and recovering the cleansed bodily fluid.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A carbonaceous material comprising at least 80 wt. % pure carbon and a functional group, wherein the functional group has been chemically-associated with the carbonaceous material by ultrasonication. 2. The carbonaceous material of claim 1 wherein the functional group has a molecular weight of equal to or less than about 1000 g/mole. 3. The carbonaceous material of claim 1 wherein the functional group comprises a heteroatom. 4. The carbonaceous material of claim 1 wherein the functional group comprises an unsaturation. 5. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein the functional group comprises organic acids, organic amides, organic amines, organic esters, organic anhydrides, organic alcohols, organic acid halides (such as acid chlorides, acid bromides, etc.) organic peroxides, unsaturated carboxylic acids, esters of the unsaturated carboxylic acids, acid anhydrides, di-esters, salts, amides, imides, aromatic vinyl compounds hydrolyzable unsaturated silane compounds and unsaturated halogenated hydrocarbons. 6. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein the functional group comprises males c anhydride, citraconic anhydride, 2-methyl maleic anhydride, 2-chloromaleic anhydride, 2,3 -dimethylmaleic anhydride, bicyclo[2.2.1]-5-heptene-2,3-dicarboxylic anhydride and 4~methyl-4-cyc3ohexene-l,2-dicarboxylic anhydride, acrylic acid, methacrylic acid, maleic acid, fumaric acid, itaconic acid, citraconic acid, mesaconic acid, crotonic acid, bicyclo[2.2.2]oct-5-ene- 2,3-di carboxylic acid anhydride, 1,2,3,4,5,8,9, 10-octahydronaphthalene-2,3-dicarboxy3ic acid anhydride, 2-oxa-l,3-diketospiro[4.4]non-7-ene, bicyclo[2.2. l]hept-5-ene-2,3-di carboxylic acid anhydride, maleopimaric acid, tetrahydrophtalic anhydride, norbom-5-ene-2,3-dicarboxylic acid anhydride, nadic anhydride, methyl nadic anhydride, himic anhydride, methyl himic anhydride, and x-methyl-bicyclo[2.2.1]hept-5-ene-2,3-dicarboxylic acid anhydride (XMNA) or combinations thereof. 7. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein the functional group comprises methyl acrylate, ethyl acrylate, butyl acrylate, methyl methacry ate, ethyl methacrylate, butyl methacrylate or combinations thereof. 8. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 having equal to or greater than about 10% of the original porosity of the carbonaceous material. 9. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 having less than about 1.0 wt.% of microparticles based on the original weight of the carbonaceous material. 10. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 having macropores ranging in size from about 0.05 Î¼Î¹Î· to about 1.0 Î¼Î¹Î· in effective diameter. 11. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein the functional group comprises a polymerizable monomer. 12. The carbonaceous material of claim 11 wherein the monomer comprises ethylene, ethylene glycol, propylene, acrylates, methyl methacrylate, n-butyl acrylate, vinyl acetates, acrylamide, ethylene oxide, or combinations thereof. 13. The carbonaceous material of any of claims 1 through 4 wherein the functional group comprises an oligomer or polymer. 14. The carbonaceous material of claim 13 wherein the functional group comprises greater than about 80 wt.% oligomer. 15. A method comprising: obtaining an un cleansed bodily fluid from a subject; contacting the uncleansed bodily fluid with a carbonaceous material comprising at least 80 wt. % pure carbon and a functional group, wherein the functional group has been chemically- associated with the carbonaceous material by ultrasonication; obtaining a cleansed bodily fluid subsequent to the contacting; and recovering the cleansed bodily fluid. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein the uncleansed bodily fluid comprises whole blood, diluted blood, heparinized blood, plasma, frozen plasma, cerebral liquid, or combinations thereof. 17. The method of claim 16 wherein the uncleansed bodily fluid comprises disease mediators. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the disease mediators comprise nitric oxide, nitrogen dioxide, nitrate, hydrogen peroxide; billirubin, cretinine, heme; LPS-endotoxin; vascular 

 endothelia growth factor, epidermal cell-derived factor, fibroblast growth factor, nerve growth factor, growth-regulated protein alpha, Interferon-inducible T-cell alpha chemoattractant, stem cell factor, transforming growth factor beta; C-reactive protein, transforming growth factor-alpha, interleukin 1-beta; interleukin-1, interleukin-2, interleukin-4, interleukin-6, interleukin 12, interleukin- 17; cholesterol, high-density lipoproteins, low-density lipoproteins, very low-density lipoproteins, triglycerides; liver enzymes, alanine transaminase, aspartate transaminase, or combinations thereof. 19. The method of claim 17 wherein the disease mediators are present in the uncleansed bodily fluid in an amount designated x and the disease mediators are present in the cleansed bodily fluid in an amount designated y. 20. The method of claim 19 wherein y has a value that is that is from about 10% to about 90% less than x. 21. The method of claim 15 wherein the subject is experiencing systemic inflammatory response syndrome. 22. The method of claim 15 wherein the subject is experiencing chronic kidney disease. 23. The method of claim 15 wherein the subject is experiencing hepatic encephalopathy.
